title: tf
summary: Create a SISO transfer-function object from numerator and denominator coefficient vectors.

gpu_support.notes: `tf` constructs host-side metadata. If coefficient inputs are gpuArray values, RunMat gathers them before creating the object.

fusion.notes: `tf` is not fused; it constructs a host-side object.

description: `tf(num, den)` creates a scalar-input scalar-output transfer-function object from numeric numerator and denominator coefficient vectors. Coefficients follow MATLAB's polynomial convention: the first entry is the highest-order term and the last entry is the constant term.
behaviors: Accepts numeric scalar, row-vector, or column-vector numerator coefficients.
Accepts numeric scalar, row-vector, or column-vector denominator coefficients.
Normalizes stored numerator and denominator coefficients to row vectors on the returned `tf` object.
Returns a lightweight object whose class name is `tf`.
Stores `Numerator`, `Denominator`, `Variable`, `Ts`, `InputDelay`, and `OutputDelay` properties on the object.
Uses continuous-time defaults: `Variable` is `'s'` and `Ts` is `0`.
`tf(num, den, Ts)` stores a finite non-negative sample time and defaults `Variable` to `'z'`.
`tf(num, den, 'Ts', Ts)` or `tf(num, den, 'SampleTime', Ts)` stores a finite non-negative sample time.
Complex coefficients are accepted and stored as complex row vectors.
Logical and integer coefficients are promoted to double precision.

examples.description: Creating a first-order continuous-time transfer function

faqs.question: Does `tf` evaluate the transfer function at frequency points?
faqs.answer: No. This implementation only constructs the transfer-function object. Frequency-response helpers such as `bode`, `freqresp`, and `evalfr` are separate Control System Toolbox functions.

faqs.question: Does `tf` support MIMO transfer functions?
faqs.answer: Not yet. This implementation supports scalar-input scalar-output systems created from numeric coefficient vectors.

faqs.question: Does `tf` implement `step`, `impulse`, or `bode`?
faqs.answer: No. Those functions are not part of this builtin.

faqs.question: Can I pass matrices of coefficients?
faqs.answer: No. Numerator and denominator inputs must be scalars or vectors.

faqs.question: Can the denominator be all zeros?
faqs.answer: No. The denominator coefficient vector must contain at least one non-zero value.

example.input:
H = tf(num, den)
H = tf(num, den, Ts)
H = tf(num, den, 'Variable', variable)
H = tf(num, den, 'Ts', Ts)

gpu_residency: `tf` is a host-side object constructor. gpuArray coefficient inputs are gathered before object construction, and the returned transfer-function object does not live on the GPU.
